title: lippia rehmannii
intro: Lippia rehmannii, also known as the Wild Lavender or Fever Grass, is a perennial herb native to tropical Africa. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Lippia rehmannii include treating fever, headaches, and digestive issues, as well as being used as a natural remedy for skin conditions and respiratory problems. Lippia rehmannii can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and its essential oil is also used in aromatherapy and traditional medicine.
